questions: how to choose colors for design
answers: Color theory is the study of how colors work together and how they affect perception and emotions. It covers concepts like the color wheel, complementary colors, warm/cool tones, and contrast.
Color theory holo color niye science — kono color er shathe konta match kore, konta kemon mood create kore, eishob study kora. Design er jonno onek important.

Understanding color theory helps you pick palettes that evoke the right emotions. Warm colors (red, orange) feel energetic while cool colors (blue, green) feel calm. Complementary colors sit opposite on the wheel.

Color theory guides designers in creating visually harmonious designs. Key concepts include primary/secondary/tertiary colors, analogous and triadic schemes, and understanding how contrast affects readability.
